sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, output_str STRING);
DECLARE ses STRING OUTPUT OBJECT; ses := string_output ();
SELECT string_output_flush(ses), (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE output_str >= '') AS count_result;
SELECT ses, (string_output_flush(ses) >= string_output_string(ses)) AS comparison FROM test_strings;
SELECT output_str, string_output_flush(ses) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1 AND output_str >= string_output_string(ses);
CREATE TABLE test_numbers (num_id INT PRIMARY KEY, num_val FLOAT);
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_numbers WHERE num_val >= 2) AS greater_count, string_output_flush(ses) FROM test_strings;
SELECT n.num_val, s.output_str FROM test_numbers n JOIN test_strings s ON n.num_val >= CAST(s.output_str AS FLOAT) WHERE s.output_str IS NOT NULL;
SELECT output_str FROM test_strings WHERE NOT NULLIF(output_str, '') >= '';
CREATE TABLE temp_table (temp_id INT, temp_val STRING);
INSERT INTO temp_table VALUES (1, string_output_string(ses)), (2, 'Another Output');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_table WHERE string_output_flush(ses) >= output_str;
SELECT temp_val FROM temp_table HAVING COUNT(*) >= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_numbers WHERE num_val IS NOT NULL);
